publishedAbstract: When restoring a red hat Quay registry using OADP with managed object storage backed by NooBaa (ODF), the restore operation completes successfully at the Kubernetes resource level. However, Quay application pods fail to start correctly and report the following error. ~~~ DistributedStorage | Could not connect to storage local_us. Error: The AWS access key Id you provided does not exist in our records. ~~~ This issue occurs after performing a namespace level backup and restore of the Quay registry namespace using OADP.
publishedTitle: OADP restore of red hat Quay with NooBaa(ODF) managed storage results in invalid AWS access key errors
